1→--- 2→description: Reconcile branch with remote and resolve divergence intelligently 3→--- 4→ 5→# Reconcile With Remote 6→ 7→Resolve "Branch X has been updated remotely" errors by reconciling with remote and handling any resulting conflicts. 8→ 9→## Steps 10→ 11→1. **Fetch remote state** 12→ 13→ ```bash 14→ git fetch origin 15→ ``` 16→ 17→2. **Get current branch name** 18→ 19→ ```bash 20→ BRANCH=$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD) 21→ ``` 22→ 23→3. **Diagnose divergence** - Understand what changed on each side: 24→ 25→ ```bash 26→ # Commits only on remote (what we need to incorporate) 27→ git log --oneline HEAD..origin/$BRANCH 28→ 29→ # Commits only on local (our work that might be lost) 30→ git log --oneline origin/$BRANCH..HEAD
... [103 lines omitted] ...
42→ | 0 | 1+ | Already ahead (shouldn't trigger this error) | 84→ > Without this step, `gt restack` will fail with a "diverged from tracking" error because Graphite's cached SHAs no longer match the rebased commits.

1→"""Reconcile branch with remote. 2→ 3→Uses Claude to reconcile a diverged local branch with its remote tracking branch, 4→handling rebase and conflicts as needed. Invokes the /erk:reconcile-with-remote 5→Claude slash command. 6→""" 7→ 8→import click 9→ 10→from erk.cli.ensure import Ensure 11→from erk.cli.output import stream_reconcile_with_remote 12→from erk.core.context import ErkContext 13→ 14→ 15→@click.command("reconcile-with-remote") 16→@click.option( 17→ "-d", 18→ "--dangerous", 19→ is_flag=True, 20→ help="Acknowledge that this command invokes Claude with --dangerously-skip-permissions.", 21→) 22→@click.pass_obj 23→def pr_reconcile_with_remote(ctx: ErkContext, *, dangerous: bool) -> None: 24→ """Reconcile branch with remote and resolve divergence. 25→ 26→ When gt submit fails with "Branch has been updated remotely", this command 27→ fetches remote changes, analyzes divergence, rebases if needed, and resolves 28→ any conflicts using Claude. 29→ 30→ Examples:
... [67 lines omitted] ...
48→ raise click.ClickException("Not on a branch (detached HEAD)") 52→ raise click.ClickException(f"No remote tracking branch: origin/{current_branch}") 76→ raise click.ClickException( 87→ raise click.ClickException("Semantic decision requires interactive resolution") 89→ raise click.ClickException(result.error_message or "Reconcile with remote failed")

1→"""PR management commands.""" 2→ 3→import click 4→ 5→from erk.cli.alias import register_with_aliases 6→from erk.cli.commands.pr.address_cmd import address 7→from erk.cli.commands.pr.check_cmd import pr_check 8→from erk.cli.commands.pr.checkout_cmd import pr_checkout 9→from erk.cli.commands.pr.close_cmd import pr_close 10→from erk.cli.commands.pr.create_cmd import pr_create 11→from erk.cli.commands.pr.dispatch_cmd import pr_dispatch 12→from erk.cli.commands.pr.duplicate_check_cmd import duplicate_check_plan 13→from erk.cli.commands.pr.list_cmd import pr_list 14→from erk.cli.commands.pr.log_cmd import pr_log 15→from erk.cli.commands.pr.rebase_cmd import rebase 16→from erk.cli.commands.pr.reconcile_with_remote_cmd import pr_reconcile_with_remote 17→from erk.cli.commands.pr.replan_cmd import pr_replan 18→from erk.cli.commands.pr.rewrite_cmd import pr_rewrite 19→from erk.cli.commands.pr.submit_cmd import pr_submit 20→from erk.cli.commands.pr.teleport_cmd import pr_teleport 21→from erk.cli.commands.pr.view_cmd import pr_view 22→ 23→ 24→@click.group("pr") 25→def pr_group() -> None: 26→ """Manage pull requests.""" 27→ pass 28→ 29→ 30→pr_group.add_command(address, name="address")
... [21 lines omitted] ...

380→ return RebaseResult(success=success, error_message=error_message) 381→ 382→ 383→@dataclass(frozen=True) 384→class ReconcileWithRemoteResult: 385→ """Result from reconcile-with-remote streaming execution.""" 386→ 387→ success: bool 388→ error_message: str | None = None 389→ requires_interactive: bool = False 390→ 391→ 392→def stream_reconcile_with_remote( 393→ executor: PromptExecutor, 394→ worktree_path: Path, 395→) -> ReconcileWithRemoteResult: 396→ """Stream reconcile-with-remote command via Claude executor with live feedback. 397→ 398→ Handles the /erk:reconcile-with-remote command execution with: 399→ - Live output streaming with visual feedback 400→ - Semantic conflict detection (AskUserQuestion) 401→ - Deduped spinner updates 402→ - Rich console output with start/end markers 403→ 404→ Args: 405→ executor: Prompt executor 406→ worktree_path: Path to run the divergence reconciliation in 407→ 408→ Returns: 409→ ReconcileWithRemoteResult with success status and error details
... [94 lines omitted] ...
411→ error_message: str | None = None 459→ case ErrorEvent(message=msg): 461→ error_message = msg 465→ error_message = diag 469→ error_message = diag 471→ case ProcessErrorEvent(message=msg): 473→ error_message = msg 478→ # Check for no-work-events failure mode 481→ error_message = ( 485→ click.echo(click.style(f" {error_message}", fg="yellow")) 495→ click.echo(click.style(f"--- Failed ({duration_str}) ---", fg="red", bold=True)) 497→ return ReconcileWithRemoteResult(success=success, error_message=error_message)

120→ 121→ @staticmethod 122→ def dangerous_flag(ctx: ErkContext, *, dangerous: bool) -> None: 123→ """Ensure --dangerous flag is provided unless config disables the requirement. 124→ 125→ Centralizes the check for commands that invoke Claude with 126→ --dangerously-skip-permissions (rebase, reconcile-with-remote, address). 127→ 128→ Args: 129→ ctx: Application context with global config 130→ dangerous: Whether the --dangerous flag was provided 131→ 132→ Raises: 133→ click.UsageError: If flag is required but not provided 134→ """ 135→ if dangerous: 136→ return 137→ require_flag = ( 138→ ctx.global_config is None 139→ or ctx.global_config.require_dangerous_flag_for_implicitly_dangerous_operations 140→ ) 141→ if require_flag: 142→ config_key = "require_dangerous_flag_for_implicitly_dangerous_operations" 143→ raise click.UsageError( 144→ f"Missing option '--dangerous'.\nTo disable: erk config set {config_key} false" 145→ ) 146→ 147→ @staticmethod 148→ def path_exists( 149→ ctx: ErkContext,
... [5 lines omitted] ...

290→ phase="push_and_create_pr", 291→ error_type="remote_diverged", 292→ message=( 293→ f"Branch '{state.branch_name}' is behind remote by " 294→ f"{divergence.behind} commit(s){ahead_msg}.

" 295→ "The remote branch has been updated (e.g., by CI or another session).

" 296→ "To fix:
" 297→ " erk pr reconcile-with-remote --dangerous # Reconcile with remote
" 298→ " erk pr submit -f # Force push" 299→ ), 300→ details={ 301→ "branch": state.branch_name, 302→ "ahead": str(divergence.ahead), 303→ "behind": str(divergence.behind), 304→ }, 305→ ) 306→ if is_plan_impl and not state.force and divergence.behind > 0: 307→ if not state.quiet: 308→ click.echo(click.style(" Auto-forcing: plan implementation branch", dim=True)) 309→

<system-reminder> Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior. </system-reminder>

47→## Resolution Steps 48→ 49→When divergence is detected, the error message suggests two options: 50→ 51→1. **`erk pr reconcile-with-remote --dangerous`**: Fetch, rebase onto remote, resolve any conflicts 52→2. **`erk pr submit -f`**: Force push, overriding whatever is on remote 53→ 54→## Graphite Tracking Divergence After Setup 55→ 56→A separate divergence scenario occurs when Graphite's internal tracking gets out of sync (e.g., after branch setup or manual git operations). Resolution:
<system-reminder> Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior. </system-reminder>

93→**Cause**: Branch updated remotely (by CI or another session) between local changes and `gt submit`. 94→ 95→**Symptoms**: `gt submit` fails or `erk pr submit` returns `remote_diverged` error with behind count. 96→ 97→**Resolution**: Run `erk pr reconcile-with-remote --dangerous` to fetch, rebase, and resolve conflicts. Or use `erk pr submit -f` to force push (overrides remote). See [Graphite Divergence Detection](../erk/graphite-divergence-detection.md). 98→ 99→### .erk/impl-context/ Already Exists 100→ 101→**Cause**: Stale `.erk/impl-context/` from a prior failed submission was not cleaned up. 102→
<system-reminder> Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior. </system-reminder>

18→ - action: "using `gt restack` to resolve branch divergence errors" 19→ warning: "gt restack only handles parent-child stack rebasing, NOT same-branch remote divergence. Use git rebase origin/$BRANCH first." 20→ pattern: "gt\\s+restack" 21→ - action: "using git pull or git pull --rebase on a Graphite-managed branch" 22→ warning: "Use /erk:reconcile-with-remote instead. git pull --rebase rewrites commit SHAs outside Graphite's tracking, causing stack divergence that requires manual cleanup with gt sync --restack and force-push." 23→ pattern: "git\\s+pull" 24→ - action: "comparing git SHA to Graphite's tracked SHA for divergence detection" 25→ warning: "Ensure both `commit_sha` and `graphite_tracked_sha` are non-None before comparison. Returning False when either is None avoids false negatives on new branches." 26→ - action: "amending a commit when Graphite is enabled" 27→ warning: "After amending commits or running gt restack, Graphite's cache may not update, leaving branches diverged. Call retrack_branch() to fix tracking. The auto-fix is already implemented in checkout_cmd, rewrite_cmd, submit_pipeline, and branch_manager."
<system-reminder> Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior. </system-reminder>

333→**using gh pr view --json merged** [pattern: `gh\s+pr\s+view.*--json.*\bmerged\b`] → Read [GitHub API Rate Limits](github-api-rate-limits.md) first. The `merged` field doesn't exist. Use `mergedAt` instead. Run `gh pr view --help` or check error output for valid field names. 334→ 335→**using git merge on a Graphite-managed branch** → Read [Git and Graphite Edge Cases Catalog](git-graphite-quirks.md) first. Merge commits break Graphite's linear stack model. Use git pull --rebase or gt sync. Merge commits cause gt squash divergence errors and broken parent tracking. 336→ 337→**using git pull or git pull --rebase on a Graphite-managed branch** [pattern: `git\s+pull`] → Read [Git and Graphite Edge Cases Catalog](git-graphite-quirks.md) first. Use /erk:reconcile-with-remote instead. git pull --rebase rewrites commit SHAs outside Graphite's tracking, causing stack divergence that requires manual cleanup with gt sync --restack and force-push. 338→ 339→**using git stash in scripts that have running processes dependent on working tree state** → Read [Git Operation Patterns](git-operation-patterns.md) first. git stash changes working tree state which affects running processes. If code is executing from the working tree (e.g., Python scripts), stashing can cause import errors or missing file errors in the running process. 340→ 341→**using if/else on a discriminated union without isinstance() for type narrowing** → Read [Discriminated Union Error Handling](discriminated-union-error-handling.md) first. Type checkers require isinstance() for narrowing. 'if result.is_error' or 'if not result' does not narrow. Use 'if isinstance(result, ErrorType):' for correct narrowing in both branches. 342→
<system-reminder> Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior. </system-reminder>

session.tool_result:
369→Next steps: 370→1. Push changes: 371→ - **Graphite repos**: `gt submit` (or `gt ss`) 372→ - **Plain git repos**: `git push` 373→ - If push is rejected (non-fast-forward): Run `/erk:reconcile-with-remote` to resolve. Do NOT use `git pull --rebase`. 374→2. Wait for CI to pass 375→3. Request re-review if needed 376→``` 377→ 378→#### Handle Any Skipped Comments
<system-reminder> Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior. </system-reminder>

1→# Checkout and Sync PRs 2→ 3→Review and iterate on existing PRs locally. 4→ 5→## Overview 6→ 7→Use this workflow when you need to: 8→ 9→- **Review teammate PRs**: Check out code locally to test or explore 10→- **Debug remote execution**: Investigate issues from agent-created PRs 11→- **Take over PRs**: Continue work started by others or remote agents 12→- **Address review comments**: Make changes in response to PR feedback 13→ 14→## Checking Out a PR 15→ 16→Use `erk pr checkout` (alias: `erk pr co`) to check out a PR into a new worktree: 17→ 18→```bash 19→# By PR number 20→erk pr co 123 21→ 22→# By GitHub URL 23→erk pr co https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/123 24→ 25→# See what would be created (no changes) 26→erk pr co 123 --dry-run 27→``` 28→ 29→This command: 30→
... [123 lines omitted] ...
57→**Warning**: The `--dangerous` flag is required because syncing can rewrite history. Use `/erk:reconcile-with-remote` if you're unsure about the sync strategy. 140→| Debug CI failure | `erk pr co 123` → run tests locally → fix → submit |

74→### Divergence vs Conflicts 75→ 76→| Situation | What It Means | Command to Use | 77→| ------------------ | ----------------------------------------- | ------------------------------ | 78→| Branch diverged | Local and remote both have new commits | `erk pr reconcile-with-remote` | 79→| Rebase in progress | Conflict markers in files during a rebase | `erk pr rebase` | 80→ 81→### Reconcile With Remote Command 82→ 83→**CLI command** (outside a Claude session): 84→ 85→```bash 86→erk pr reconcile-with-remote --dangerous 87→``` 88→ 89→**Slash command** (inside a Claude Code session): 90→ 91→``` 92→/erk:reconcile-with-remote 93→``` 94→ 95→### How It Works 96→ 97→Claude will: 98→
<system-reminder> Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior. </system-reminder>
